sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a strong attacking display in their recent 3-1 victory over Elche. Valencia, on the other hand, are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches, including a heavy 6-0 loss to Barcelona, reflecting defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, Alavés have the upper hand with 1 win and 1 draw against Valencia, including a 1-0 home victory in May 2025. Historically, matches between these teams tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés, positioned 11th with 11 points, boast a solid defense (fewest goals conceded in the league) and average 6.5 corners per game, ranking 3rd. Valencia, sitting 16th with 8 points, have struggled offensively and defensively, conceding 14 goals while scoring only 10 this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Alavés’ strong home form and defensive stability give them an edge, especially against a Valencia side lacking consistency. Valencia’s inability to keep clean sheets (3 consecutive games) and Alavés’ tendency to score late could be decisive factors.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 46% chance for a home win, combined with Alavés’ superior recent form and head-to-head dominance, they are favored to win. Expect a tight, low-scoring game with a high likelihood of over 4.5 yellow cards, considering Alavés’ streak of card-heavy matches.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Deportivo Alavés and Valencia at Estadio de Mendizorroza ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Alavés dominated possession and showed more intent in the final third, while Valencia struggled to create meaningful opportunities. Despite the scoreline, the home side looked more likely to score based on their attacking presence and entries into dangerous areas. The away side, however, remained resilient defensively, limiting clear-cut chances for Alavés.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance: Deportivo Alavés
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés controlled much of the game, enjoying a significant share of the ball and frequently penetrating the final third. Their ability to win duels and aerial battles was evident, showcasing physical dominance over Valencia. Players like Lucas Boyé and Pablo Ibáñez stood out with their contributions in attack, getting shots on target and creating openings. However, Alavés lacked the clinical edge needed to convert their dominance into goals. Defensively, they were solid, with players like Jon Pacheco and Nahuel Tenaglia making key clearances and interceptions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance: Valencia
halftimeSections.text: Valencia had a tougher first half, with less possession and fewer entries into the attacking third. Their defensive structure held firm, though, as they managed to frustrate Alavés' attacking efforts. Players like César Tárrega were pivotal at the back, making important clearances to keep the scoreline level. In attack, Valencia showed occasional promise, with Arnaut Danjuma managing a shot on target, but overall, they lacked the cohesion and penetration to seriously threaten the Alavés defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw distinct shifts in attacking momentum between the two sides. Early on, Valencia started with some intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Alavés quickly took over, showing sustained attacking spells for much of the half, especially in the middle and latter stages. Their persistence in building pressure was notable, with several prolonged periods of dominance in the opponent’s half. Valencia had brief moments of counter-attacking threat, particularly around the midway point, but couldn’t maintain momentum as Alavés reasserted control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Alavés, the focus in the second half will be on sharpening their finishing. They’ve created enough opportunities but need to be more decisive in front of goal. Defensively, they must remain vigilant against potential counters from Valencia. On the other hand, Valencia needs to improve their ball retention and build more structured attacks to relieve pressure on their defense. They’ll also look to exploit any gaps left by Alavés if the home side pushes too aggressively for a goal.
